title: Flow of Exception Handling with Multiple Catch Clauses
description: A flowchart depicting how a try block's potential exceptions are evaluated against a series of catch clauses in sequence, with the first matching clause being executed.
elements: Try Block (Code that may throw exceptions)
relationships: Flow proceeds from Start into the Try Block
If an exception is thrown, it is compared sequentially to Catch Clause 1, then 2, etc.
The first catch clause whose exception type matches the thrown exception is executed
After any catch block executes, flow proceeds to the Finally Block (if present)
After the Finally Block, flow continues to the Next Statement
If no catch clause matches, the exception propagates to the caller, potentially leading to Program Termination
